name: Pendón River Gorges Trail
description: Hike the Foces del Rio Pendon circular route, a moderate 5.4-mile trail through dramatic river gorges and lush Atlantic forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Nava offers diverse natural landscapes in Eastern Asturias, characterized by rolling hills, verdant meadows, and dense Atlantic forests. The region is framed by the Sierra de Peñamayor mountain range, providing a dramatic backdrop for many trails. Notable features include the Foces del Río Pendón, a system of dramatic river gorges, and various peaks such as Pico Texíu. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking experiences for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Nava The most popular hiking route is Pendón River Gorges Trail, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through dramatic river gorges and lush Atlantic forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pendón River Gorges – Waterfall in a Lush Forest loop from Nava, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path. This trail explores the Foces del Río Pendón, featuring a waterfall within a dense forest environment. Local hikers also love the Pico Texíu – collado Llagos loop from Nava, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and offering views of Pico Texíu, often completed in about 4 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Nava is defined by river gorges, dense forests, and mountain ranges like Sierra de Peñamayor. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding mountain climbs. The routes in Nava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Nava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Nava?
A: Nava, located in Eastern Asturias, offers a diverse range of natural landscapes. You'll encounter rolling hills, verdant meadows, and dense Atlantic forests. The dramatic Sierra de Peñamayor mountain range provides a stunning backdrop, and the Foces del Río Pendón features impressive river gorges. Almost any road in Asturias, including those around Nava, will immerse visitors in dreamlike scenery.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for all skill levels in Nava?
A: Yes, Nava provides a wide array of hiking routes for various fitness levels. There are 5 easy trails for leisurely strolls, 12 moderate routes requiring good fitness, and 5 more demanding trails for experienced hikers. Whether you're looking for a short walk or a challenging mountain climb, you'll find options here.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Nava?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Nava are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pendón River Gorges – Waterfall in a Lush Forest loop from Nava&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24642125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Pra loop from Nava&lt;/a&gt; offers an easier, shorter circular walk.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls in Nava?
A: Yes, the Foces del Río Pendón area is known for its lush Atlantic forests and river features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pendón River Gorges – Waterfall in a Lush Forest loop from Nava&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a waterfall within a dense forest environment, making it a great choice for those seeking this natural attraction.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on hikes around Nava?
A: Beyond the stunning river gorges of the Foces del Río Pendón and the Sierra de Peñamayor, you can explore various peaks and passes. Highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful area that serves as a crossing point to surrounding peaks, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/337916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gavío or Cimero&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter traditional Asturian houses and rural churches along the routes.

Q: Is Nava a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Nava offers several easy trails perfect for families. These routes typically involve less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing an enjoyable experience for all ages. The region's verdant meadows and serene environment make for pleasant family outings.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Nava?
A: Nava's oceanic climate, influenced by the Atlantic, contributes to lush greenery year-round. While hiking is possible throughout the year, early autumn is particularly special for witnessing the rutting of deer in the Sierra de Peñamayor. Spring and early summer also offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant flora.

Q: Are there options for public transport to reach hiking trailheads in Nava?
A: While specific public transport details for individual trailheads can vary, Nava is a municipality in Asturias, and local bus services connect it to larger towns. For reaching more remote trailheads, personal vehicles are often more convenient. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes to Nava town and then plan accordingly for trail access.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Nava?
A: The hiking routes in Nava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, from dramatic river gorges to lush forests and mountain views. The variety of trails catering to different abilities is also a common highlight.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails in Nava?
A: Yes, Nava has 5 designated easy hiking trails. An excellent option for a relaxed walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24642125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Pra loop from Nava&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 4.8 km (3 miles) long and takes around 1 hour 25 minutes to complete, with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Nava?
A: Yes, the region has a charming rural character. Notably, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt; area features a mountain-style bar and restaurant, offering a place to refuel. The town of Nava itself is famous for its cider production and has various cafes and restaurants where you can enjoy local Asturian cuisine.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Nava?
A: While specific regulations can vary by trail or protected area, generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Nava, especially if kept on a leash. It's always a good practice to ensure your dog is under control and to clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.
tips: Fuensanta began as a spring of water and baths whose use dates back to the Romans, but the first written testimonies are from medieval times. The site has a lot of history, such as the discovery of the Nuevo Manantial in 1868, the installation of electricity in the spa in 1893, the start of the sale of bottled water in Asturian pharmacies in 1913, the launch of food formats in 1983 and the beginning of its international expansion to the United States and Puerto Rico in 1985. In 1968 it began to sell in hospitality and food, already having the fastest water packaging line in Spain. Near the spring there is a route (''Foces del río Banner'') of approximately 10 km. The path is circular: it begins and ends in Fuensanta and passes through Collau de la Canal and Mayao Pastor.
Nice waterfall in a very green environment.
The Banner River is born in the Sierra de Peñamayor. Already in its upper course, at the Bridge of the Banner River, it receives the waters of the Bomalu stream.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail?
faqs.answer: The Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail usually takes between 3 to 5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery. The total distance is approximately 8.7 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike, and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally rated as having a low to medium difficulty, making it suitable for families and individuals who are accustomed to walking. While there's a continuous, progressive ascent in the initial half, it's manageable and doesn't require technical expertise. Some sections might have slippery stones, especially when wet, but the path is generally wide and safe.

faqs.question: Where does the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail start, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently begins in Fuensanta, near a former spa that now operates as a mineral water bottling plant. Parking is often available in this area, making it an accessible starting point for your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, offering a rich experience. You'll walk on ascending forest tracks shaded by ancient chestnut trees, and through dramatic gorges carved by the Río Pendón. Within these 'foces,' the path is frequently hewn into the limestone rock, running just meters from the river. You'll also encounter open pastoral areas with traditional cabins and enjoy panoramic views of the Pra valley.

faqs.question: What natural features and highlights can I see along the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in natural beauty. You'll experience impressive geological formations within the Pendón River Gorges, a lush Atlantic forest with ancient trees, and unique plant species like the *Woodwardia radicans* fern. Keep an eye out for the Waterfall in a Lush Forest and the Fuensanta Natural Mineral Spring near the start.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly beautiful. In spring, the forest is vibrant with new growth, and in autumn, the chestnut and oak trees display stunning colors. Summers are pleasant due to the shade provided by the dense forest, while winter offers a quieter, often misty, atmosphere, though some sections might be wetter.

faqs.question: Is the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or when passing through narrow gorge sections, to ensure the safety of your pet and other hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail is recognized as one of the "Paths of the Cider Region" (*Senderos de la Comarca de la Sidra*). The route also covers 100% of the official "Foces del Río Pendón" trail and sections of the "PR-AS-45 Foces del Río Pendón" path.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Foces del Rio Pendon — circular trail?
faqs.answer: We recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes, as some sections can be slippery, especially after rain. Pack enough water and snacks, as there are no facilities directly on the trail. Depending on the weather, a light rain jacket and layers are advisable. Don't forget your camera to capture the stunning gorges and lush forest!

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for hiking this route?
faqs.answer: While generally moderate, the initial half involves a continuous, progressive ascent. Be mindful of slippery stones, particularly in the gorge sections or after rainfall. Starting early can help you avoid crowds, especially during peak seasons. The trail's circular design makes navigation straightforward, but following the signage is always a good idea.
